primary_accounting_issue: Whether an intragroup dividend receivable creating foreign exchange risk exposure can be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements under IFRS hedge accounting rules

primary_authority.reason: Establishes the fundamental rule that only transactions with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements

Provides the specific exception allowing foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged item in consolidated statements when exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies that intragroup monetary items cannot be eliminated against corresponding intragroup items without showing currency fluctuation results in consolidated financial statements
Defines what constitutes a monetary item receivable from a foreign operation and when it is considered part of net investment in that foreign operation

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses hedges of net investments in foreign operations but provides guidance on different hedging scenarios than intragroup monetary items
General hedge accounting requirements but does not specifically address eligibility of intragroup items for hedge designation

treatment_families.family: Eligibility of intragroup monetary items as hedged items for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Hedge accounting permitted for foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: When the intragroup dividend receivable is between entities with different functional currencies, the resulting foreign exchange exposure is not fully eliminated on consolidation per IAS 21 paragraph 45, and may qualify as a hedged item under the exception in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6

approaches.label: Hedge accounting not permitted in consolidated statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: When the intragroup dividend receivable is between entities with the same functional currency, any foreign exchange differences are fully eliminated on consolidation and the exposure does not affect consolidated profit or loss, making the item ineligible for hedge designation in consolidated financial statements per IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5
